programmes.show_description: Ceramics expert Kirsten and clockwork king Steve work their magic on an Indian dancing doll. Plus, the team revive a musical menorah and a handmade sewing box.
A high-rise construction project in Virginia collapses. In the UK, a rural roadway is destroyed, and a ship takes down a bridge in Georgia, USA.
A race car slams into a wall at 100mph; multiple homes are destroyed by 260mph winds; a 70-year-old pilot crash lands; and a van smashes into a police car at 70 mph.
A freight train smashes into a semi-truck, a four storey building collapses, a motorcycle stunt goes badly wrong and a house smashes into a bridge during floods.
See a dramatic train explosion, storm chasers on the edge, a window platform smash into a building, vehicles colliding at The Baja 500 and a spectacular helicopter crash.
Firefighters are caught in an explosion, warheads explode in a tunnel, an avalanche buries an entire city and a race car snaps in half at 310mph.
Holograms, package printing and canned sweetcorn. More everyday objects are examined to see how they were made.
Plastic bags, solar panels, petrol containers. More everyday objects are examined to see how they were made.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
The restorers go global with artefacts from around the world. They include a 200-year-old guitar from Vienna and a '70s pinball machine.
Educating Al. Drew teaches Al about buying from sources off limits to the competition. They nab a collection of crucibles and patterns at a Lancashire foundry.
Firsts For Viki. Drew takes Viki to three types of hunting grounds that have been staples of his business. They snap up a rare Anglepoise lamp along the way.
Permit delays force the Poseidon Crew off road as they try to survive the season. Plus, the Dust Devils gamble big in the hope of finding fine gold.
Marcus and Linden fear a flat battery could leave them stuck in the outback for days. Plus, Shane and Russell attempt to get their season on track.
A new road trip sees Steven Moore find some pricey porcelain in an antique shop loo. Natasha Raskin Sharp goes down a secret tunnel on a journey back in time.
Angus and the team face the mammoth task of clearing a collection of bow top caravans. Later, a widower wants to sell a collection of stamps and Beatrix Potter figurines.
The restorers go global with artefacts from around the world. They include a 200-year-old guitar from Vienna and a '70s pinball machine.
Across The Irish Sea. The adventure in Northern Ireland continues. Henry and Sam hunt down some army vehicles, tractors and some very well-hidden land rovers.
A Royal Visit. Henry Cole and Sam Lovegrove are in the Queen's realm as they visit a shed in the shadow of Windsor Castle. Will they find a royal bargain of a motorcycle?
David Kingerley. Henry and Fuzz visit David's Derbyshire shed, a treasure trove of vintage mechanical gems, and uncover a wonderful Monet-Goyon motorbike.
Richard Gaunt. Henry and Fuzz visit Richard's Staffordshire shed, uncovering an Excelsior and Mobylette as they dive into a treasure trove of two-wheeled gems.
Actor Lisa Maxwell needs help clearing clutter from her renovated Cotswolds home. And Angus helps snooker ace Dennis Taylor sell sporting memorabilia.
Angus explores Boyzone star Shane Lynch's super-cars and a camper-van-turned-mini-bar. Then he checks out presenter Julia Bradbury's Victorian chair.
Charles Hanson and Catherine Southon start off the first leg of their trip in Sussex. Along their journey they visit Storrington, Petworth Lewes and Peacehaven.
Charles Hanson and Catherine Southon continue their trip as they scour Kent for antiques. Charles finds a pair of rare 18th-century shoe buckles he thinks may turn a profit.
Graham Streeter. Henry and Fuzz visit Graham's Essex shed, packed with bikes, and uncover a Suzuki GSX-R750 Slabside, Henry's dream machine and a true Japanese icon.
Jeff Maggs. Henry and Fuzz visit Jeff's tractor-packed shed in Chepstow and uncover a Grey Fergie, the iconic Ferguson TE20 that helped transform farming forever.

programmes.show_name: New: Shed And Buried: Classic Cars
programmes.show_description: Rio Whistler. Henry and Fuzz visit James in Burnham-on-Crouch to revive his rare Rio Whistler military vehicle. Can they return this weathered veteran to duty?
MotoGP: USA Highlights. Highlights of all the action from the MotoGP 2026 motorcycle racing World Championship at the Circuit Of The Americas in the US, near Austin, Texas.
How do they make the favourite sweet of the Ottomans, James Bond's gyroplane and the Supercat- the world's fastest ferry?
How do they carve longboards, turn millions of lemons into juice, and build industry's versatile heavy lifter, the forklift truck?
How do they roast the coffee that wakes up Italy, bake the bricks that build America, and blast diamonds from a frozen volcano?
How do they take the world's sharpest photos, stitch an airbag to catch a falling car, and produce billions of perfectly-formed jelly beans?
DeLorean. Mike has travelled to the US to hunt for a DeLorean, one of the most recognisable and distinctive cars ever made. Can Edd restore this iconic car and maximise their profit?
Chevy Truck. Mike is in San Francisco looking for a classic Chevrolet Step Side Pick Up. Will he manage to find a rust-free model for Edd to restore?
Across The Irish Sea. The adventure in Northern Ireland continues. Henry and Sam hunt down some army vehicles, tractors and some very well-hidden land rovers.
A Royal Visit. Henry Cole and Sam Lovegrove are in the Queen's realm as they visit a shed in the shadow of Windsor Castle. Will they find a royal bargain of a motorcycle?
Discover how they manufacture bicycle helmets, produce aluminium, create lithium batteries and make car brakes.
Find out how lenses, granite, crisps and computer processors are produced, as more everyday objects are put under the microscope.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
The team restore a miniature whiskey barrel. Plus, a heritage craft dating back thousands of years is put to good use repairing a set of wicker baby-weighing scales.
Forgotten Treasures. Drew snaps up a rare pair of console tables in Sherborne. Then, he visits the UK's last drop hammer tool-making forge before picking in the Cotswolds.
Key Hunting Grounds. Drew takes Viki to his favourite hunting grounds. Together, they pick through a dusty old shed, a quality auction, and a beautiful country house.
A wet plant disaster threatens the Dust Devils' potentially lucrative fine gold find. Plus, the Gold Timers desperately try to turn their season around.
The Gold Gypsies could be sitting on a fortune but must decide whether to take a huge gamble. A water shortage halts Shane and Russell's operations.
Northumberland sees Natasha on safari tracking ancient creatures while Steven finds an old elephant. Both experts go Georgian with their treasures in Dundee.
After clearing out a home in Leeds, the team sort through a van full of mysterious boxes. Plus, Angus checks out a retired archaeologist's beautiful collection.
The team restore a miniature whiskey barrel. Plus, a heritage craft dating back thousands of years is put to good use repairing a set of wicker baby-weighing scales.
Penny For Your Thoughts. Back in Northern Ireland, Henry and Sam discover some lovely vintage cars as well as some modern motors. What will they take home?
Henry and Sam are in the Cotswolds to visit the largest sidecar manufacturer in Britain. They uncover a rusty Lancia Aurelia and a Royal Enfield 500.
Graham Streeter. Henry and Fuzz visit Graham's Essex shed, packed with bikes, and uncover a Suzuki GSX-R750 Slabside, Henry's dream machine and a true Japanese icon.
Jeff Maggs. Henry and Fuzz visit Jeff's tractor-packed shed in Chepstow and uncover a Grey Fergie, the iconic Ferguson TE20 that helped transform farming forever.
Dominic Littlewood & Lisa George. In Dom Littlewood's stylish seaside pad, Angus finds a rare guitar. Then, he checks out 1950s treasures at soap star Lisa George's home.
Les Dennis & Sherrie Hewson. Angus unearths some family fortunes at comedy legend Les Dennis' home. Then, he checks out a selection of movie memorabilia at Sherrie Hewson's house.
Charles Hanson and Catherine Southon resume their trip in Warwickshire. Catherine finds a French copper and brass letter holder and Charles grabs a charming oak chair.
Charles Hanson and Catherine Southon embark on the penultimate leg of their trip. Charles goes to work with a metal detector while Catherine sticks to antiques stores.
Rick Kenway. Henry and Fuzz visit Richard's eclectic shed in Hook, packed with bikes, cars and curiosities, and choose a classic Harley-Davidson Sportster to save.
Dave McNicholas. Henry and Fuzz visit Dave's Derby shed and pick a classic Triumph Daytona, a British icon with racing heritage, for their next restoration challenge.
Heading In The Right Direction. The Cogs take centre stage at Britain's premiere car show, but the wheels fall off when Richard returns James May's restored Triumph Stag.
Rio Whistler. Henry and Fuzz visit James in Burnham-on-Crouch to revive his rare Rio Whistler military vehicle. Can they return this weathered veteran to duty?
How do they build a caravan, forge tuning forks, resurface an ice rink, and put on the world's biggest light festival?
How do they make the groovy vinyl LP? How do they build the dodgem? How do they make one of the world's oldest pain remedies, tiger balm?
